/home/erik/go/pkg/mod/github.com/!document!driven!d!x/fizeau@v0.10.0/.ddx/beads.jsonl:211:{"acceptance":"1. Per-provider state machine: available -> quota_exhausted -> available with retry_after timestamp. 2. Burn-rate tracking with operator-configurable per-provider budget. 3. Header parsing for at least Anthropic + OpenAI + OpenRouter quota headers. 4. Periodic HealthCheck probe restores quota_exhausted providers. 5. NoViableProviderForNow typed error with retry_after + exhausted_providers fields. 6. Existing tests pass; new tests cover state transitions, header parsing, probe behavior, NoViableProviderForNow shape. 7. CONTRACT-003 amended to document the new state, error, and operator config knobs.","created_at":"2026-05-02T18:05:43.402325681Z","description":"Auto-routing today handles per-request transient feedback (RecordRouteAttempt; CONTRACT-003 line 1413) but does NOT track per-provider quota state across requests. Result: when a provider hits its subscription/daily cap, every subsequent dispatch fails until operator notices. The 'point of auto-routing' is exactly this: take a quota-exhausted provider out of rotation, keep dispatching to others, periodically recheck.

## Multi-account model (downstream operator decision, locked)

Each API key = its own provider entry in ~/.config/agent/config.yaml (e.g., 'claude-acct-1', 'claude-acct-2'). Fizeau treats them independently. No new schema for multi-key-per-provider; reuse existing provider abstraction. Operator-friendly: rotate accounts by adding entries.

## Prediction model (locked)

Combine two signals:
1. Provider-supplied headers when available (Anthropic X-RateLimit-Remaining-* family, OpenAI rate-limit headers, OpenRouter equivalents)
2. Local burn-rate tracking — Fizeau maintains rolling per-provider token-usage window, compares against operator-configured per-provider budget (e.g., providers.claude-acct-1.daily_token_budget: 1_000_000)

When EITHER signal predicts exhaustion before the next reset, take the provider out proactively (don't wait for the actual quota error).

## Behavior on take-out

- Provider transitions to 'quota_exhausted' state with an estimated retry_after timestamp (from headers when available; from window-reset estimate from local tracking otherwise)
- Routing engine excludes quota_exhausted providers from candidate selection
- Other providers continue serving requests normally
- Periodic probe (lightweight HealthCheck) at provider's retry_after OR at a fallback interval (default 5 min) returns the provider to 'available' on success

## New error class: NoViableProviderForNow

Distinct from existing 'no-candidate' / 'config' errors. Returned when Execute has zero eligible providers solely because all are quota_exhausted (i.e., would otherwise be available). Includes:
  - retry_after timestamp (earliest expected provider recovery)
  - exhausted_providers []string (which providers are out)

DDx (downstream) consumes this to pause its drain loop and resume on retry_after.

## Out of scope

- Rate-limit handling (per-second / per-minute throttle via 429 + Retry-After header) — this stays in the per-request feedback path; provider stays in rotation. DDx handles per-bead retry. Distinct from this bead.
- Auto-account-creation / billing integration. Operator manages keys.
- Cost-budget enforcement (different concern; lives in DDx --max-cost flag)","id":"agent-63bf58a6","issue_type":"epic","labels":["area:fizeau","area:routing","kind:feature","downstream:ddx"],"priority":0,"status":"open","title":"fizeau: provider quota state — burn-rate tracking, header-driven prediction, periodic probe, NoViableProviderForNow typed error","updated_at":"2026-05-02T18:06:53.670922412Z"}

Mechanical work driven by the TD's decisions:

1. Update FEAT-004 docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md line 65 — change documented status list from 3 values to the canonical 6 (open, in_progress, closed, blocked, proposed, cancelled).
2. Update SD-004 docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-004-beads-tracker.md — cross-reference the new TD.
3. Audit cli/internal/bead/types.go status constants (lines 59-75) — convert any non-canonical status references to use the canonical Status* constants.
4. Audit cli/internal/agent/ for non-canonical persisted-status writes — convert event-kind/phase/derived-category names that were misused as statuses (per the TD's naming-role decision matrix).
5. Update IsValidStatusTransition (cli/internal/bead/types.go:110-125) to match the TD's transition matrix.

NO functional behavior change. Pure documentation + code-vocabulary alignment to the TD.

Claim behavior: the auto-triage sweep is the only path that releases a
claim it does not own. It MUST log the prior `assignee` and `claimed-at`
in the appended event body.

Loop interaction: triage runs out-of-band of execute-bead. It MUST NOT
race with an active drain attempt holding the store lock — it acquires
the same store lock and releases-then-reclaims is not its job.

### 8.3 QuotaPauseContract (ddx-aede917d)

Status transitions used: none. Quota is a worker-state concern, not a
bead-state concern.

Labels: none added on the affected bead. The current attempt is treated
as `execution_failed` (claim released, status returns to `open`).

Events fired: `drain-paused-quota` is appended to a worker-scoped event
log (drain-process record), not to the bead's event stream. A
`unclaimed` event is appended to the bead.

Claim behavior: the claim on the in-flight bead is released cleanly so
another worker (or a later resumed worker) can pick it up.

Loop interaction: the worker transitions to worker-state
`paused-quota` (section 10). It stops claiming new beads until the
configured backoff elapses or an explicit resume signal arrives.

### 8.4 RateLimitRetryContract (ddx-c6e3db02)

Status transitions used: none. Rate-limit retry is internal to a single
attempt; the bead remains `in_progress` throughout.

Labels: none.

Events fired: `rate-limit-retry` per retry, on the bead's event stream.
Each event carries the retry count and the wait duration in the body.

Claim behavior: claim is held continuously across retries.

Loop interaction: rate-limit handling is bounded by the retry budget; on
budget exhaustion the outcome becomes `execution_failed` and the
standard mapping in section 5 applies. The worker briefly enters
`paused-rate-limit` for the wait window, then returns to `draining`.

### 8.5 LockContentionContract (ddx-da11a34a)

Status transitions used: none. Lock contention is a store-access
concern, not a bead-state concern.

Labels: none.

Events fired: `lock-contention` (append-only, on the affected bead, only
after the contention has been resolved and the mutation succeeded — so a
future audit can see that contention was observed). Pure read-side
contention does not emit an event.

Claim behavior: unaffected. The claim is acquired or not; partial states
are not persisted.

Loop interaction: the worker retries the locked operation with
exponential backoff up to a bounded budget. On budget exhaustion the
calling outcome maps to `execution_failed` (section 5). The worker does
not enter a dedicated worker-state for lock contention; it remains
`draining` and treats the failure as ordinary.

## 9. Future-Change Process

This is the rule of record for any subsequent change to bead state.

> Any bead that introduces or changes a label, an event kind, an outcome →
> state mapping, claim handling, or worker-state semantics MUST cite the
> TD-031 section that authorizes the change. If no section authorizes it,
> TD-031 is amended in the same PR (or a parent bead) before the dependent
> work lands.
>
> Adding a new persisted bead status is **not** authorized by TD-031.
> It requires upstream bd/br coordination AND an amendment to ADR-004.

In practice:

data.text: This bead's description and label `blocked-on-upstream:agent-63bf58a6` mark it as gated on a Fizeau release that has not yet shipped. Confirming: `fizeau v0.10.0` (the version pinned in go.mod) contains no `NoViableProviderForNow` symbol, and the upstream bead is still `status:"open"` in Fizeau's tracker. Per project policy (no `replace` directives), DDx must wait for a tagged release. Writing a no-changes rationale.

input.content:
# no_changes_rationale — ddx-aede917d

## Outcome
no_changes — blocked on upstream Fizeau release.

## Why this attempt produced no commit

This bead is explicitly marked `blocked-on-upstream:agent-63bf58a6` in
its labels and states in its description:

  > This bead is downstream of Fizeau agent-63bf58a6 — depends on its
  > tagged release. Until that lands, DDx falls back to today's
  > behavior (failing beads on quota errors).

Implementing AC1–AC9 requires a typed `NoViableProviderForNow` error
(with `retry_after` and `exhausted_providers` fields) returned from
the Fizeau dispatcher. The drain loop must be able to type-assert this
error to distinguish quota pause from `execution_failed`.

## Verification that the upstream is not yet released

1. cli/go.mod pins `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau v0.10.0`.
2. `grep -rn NoViableProviderForNow` in
   `~/go/pkg/mod/github.com/!document!driven!d!x/fizeau@v0.10.0/`
   returns no Go-source matches. The only mention is inside the
   embedded `.ddx/beads.jsonl`, where Fizeau bead `agent-63bf58a6` is
   still `status:"open"`.
3. No tagged Fizeau release containing the new error type is available
   to upgrade to.

## Why we cannot proceed via a `replace` directive

User-pinned policy (memory: feedback_no_local_replace) — DDx must
always import properly tagged agent releases; no `go.mod replace`,
even temporarily. Implementing this bead against an in-tree or
file-replace Fizeau would violate that rule and produce a commit that
cannot be merged.

## What a follow-up needs

1. Fizeau ships a tagged release (≥ v0.11.0 or a dedicated tag) that
   exports `NoViableProviderForNow` with `RetryAfter time.Time` and
   `ExhaustedProviders []string`.
2. `cli/go.mod` is bumped to that release.
3. Then implement, in the drain loop (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go
   and cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop*.go):
   - Type-assert the dispatcher error; on `*NoViableProviderForNow`,
     do NOT mark `execution_failed`. Release the claim (worker-state
     transition only — bead status unchanged per
     TD-031 §8.3 QuotaPauseContract).
   - Append `unclaimed` to the bead's event stream and append
     `drain-paused-quota` to the worker-scoped event log with
     canonical name from TD-031 §8.3.
   - Sleep until `min(retry_after, now+--quota-pause-max)`; re-probe
     by attempting the next ready bead. Backoff if still
     `NoViableProviderForNow`.
   - If `retry_after > now + --quota-pause-max`, exit with operator
     message: `"quota exhausted, expected recovery <ts>; relaunch
     when ready"`.
   - Add `--quota-pause-max` flag (default 30m) to `ddx agent
     execute-loop` and `ddx work`.
4. Surface `drain-paused-quota` worker events in
   `cli/internal/server/workers.go` so the GraphQL workers list shows
   paused workers.
5. New test (cli/cmd or cli/internal/agent): spawn 2 workers, simulate
   one provider quota-exhausting, assert claim released and sibling
   picks the bead up.
6. Confirm `schema_compat_test.go` round-trip still passes (no schema
   change is needed — TD-031 §8.3 is explicit that this is a
   worker-state concern only).

## What is done

Nothing committed. The bead remains executable as soon as the
upstream tag is published; this rationale documents the block so the
post-merge reviewer does not flag it as `no_evidence_produced`.

data.text: No commit is being made. Bead `ddx-aede917d` is `blocked-on-upstream:agent-63bf58a6`; Fizeau v0.10.0 (the pinned release) does not yet export `NoViableProviderForNow`, and project policy forbids a local `replace` directive. Wrote `.ddx/executions/20260502T192934-7a9b3770/no_changes_rationale.txt` documenting the block and the follow-up plan once Fizeau ships a tag with the typed error.
